title: Local Government : Subsidiary Legislation : Kgalagadi District Council (barber) Bye-laws
title_en: Local Government : Subsidiary Legislation : Kgalagadi District Council (barber) Bye-laws

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content:
1. Citation
These Bye-Laws may be cited as the Kgalagadi District Council (Barber) Bye-Laws*.
*Originally made under the Township Act now repealed, these regulations have been continued under s 94(2) of the Local Government Act, 2013.

provisions.title: Interpretation
Licence required to carry on business of barber
Application for licence

definitions.definition: any person authorised by the Council for the purpose of giving effect to these Bye-Laws
any person who carries on or assists in carrying on the business of trimming, or cutting the hair or beard of people, as well as carry out manicure, pedicure or other similar beauty treatments
the area within the jurisdiction of the Council
the place of business of a barber and shall include tent, booth, shed, stall or similar structure as the Council may approve.
